    44	function getCompanionRunId(config: unknown): string | null {
    45	  if (config === null || typeof config !== 'object') return null;
    46	  const companionRunId = (config as { companionRunId?: unknown }).companionRunId;
    47	  return typeof companionRunId === 'string' && companionRunId.trim() !== '' ? companionRunId : null;
    48	}
    49	
    50	function buildParsedModelData(runs: RunRow[], modelId: string): ParsedModelData {
    51	  const scenarios: ConsistencyParsedScenario[] = [];
    52	  const pairs: ConsistencyParsedPair[] = [];
    53	  let invalidShape = false;
    54	
    55	  for (const run of runs) {
    56	    for (const analysis of run.analysisResults) {
    57	      if (analysis.analysisType !== 'AGGREGATE' || analysis.status !== 'CURRENT') continue;
    58	      const rawSummary = readConsistencySummary(analysis.output);
    59	      const rawModel = rawSummary?.perModel;
    60	      const rawEntry = rawModel != null && typeof rawModel === 'object' && !Array.isArray(rawModel)
    61	        ? (rawModel as Record<string, unknown>)[modelId] ?? null
    62	        : null;
    63	      if (rawEntry == null || typeof rawEntry !== 'object') {
    64	        continue;
    65	      }
    66	
    67	      // A missing perScenario key means "no repeat coverage for this model in
    68	      // this run" — single-sample runs legitimately omit it. Only flag the row
    69	      // as invalid-summary-shape when the key is present but parses to zero
    70	      // entries (i.e. malformed data the pipeline produced).
    71	      const rawPerScenario = (rawEntry as { perScenario?: unknown }).perScenario;
    72	      if (rawPerScenario == null) {
    73	        continue;
    74	      }
    75	      const perScenario = parseScenarioList(rawPerScenario);
    76	      if (perScenario.length === 0) {
    77	        invalidShape = true;
    78	        continue;
    79	      }
    80	
    81	      scenarios.push(
    82	        ...perScenario.map((scenario) => ({
    83	          ...scenario,
    84	          domainId: run.definition?.domainId ?? scenario.domainId,
    85	          domainName: run.definition?.domain?.name ?? scenario.domainName,
    86	        })),
    87	      );
    88	
    89	      pairs.push(...parsePairList((rawEntry as { perPair?: unknown }).perPair));
    90	    }
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  return { scenarios, pairs, invalidShape };
    94	}
    95	
    96	function buildRepeatabilitySummary(scenarios: ConsistencyParsedScenario[]): ModelsConsistencyShape['models'][number]['repeatability'] {
    97	  const repeatability = computeRepeatability(scenarios);
    98	  const perDomainMap = new Map<string, ConsistencyParsedScenario[]>();
    99	  for (const scenario of scenarios) {
   100	    const key = `${scenario.domainId}::${scenario.domainName}`;
   101	    const list = perDomainMap.get(key) ?? [];
   102	    list.push(scenario);
   103	    perDomainMap.set(key, list);
   104	  }
   105	
   106	  return {
   107	    ...repeatability,
   108	    perDomain: [...perDomainMap.entries()]
   109	      .map(([key, rows]) => {
   110	        const [rawDomainId, rawDomainName] = key.split('::');
   111	        const pooled = computeRepeatability(rows);
   112	        return {
   113	          domainId: rawDomainId ?? 'unknown',
   114	          domainName: rawDomainName ?? 'Unknown domain',
   115	          value: pooled.value,
   116	          ciLow: pooled.ciLow,
   117	          ciHigh: pooled.ciHigh,
   118	          scenariosMeasured: rows.length,
   119	        };
   120	      })
   121	      .sort((left, right) => right.scenariosMeasured - left.scenariosMeasured || left.domainName.localeCompare(right.domainName)),
   122	  };
   123	}
   124	
   125	function buildCoherenceSummary(pairs: ConsistencyParsedPair[]): ModelsConsistencyShape['models'][number]['coherence'] {
   126	  const perPair = pairs.map((pair) => ({
   127	    domainId: pair.domainId,
   128	    valueKey: pair.valueKey,
   129	    rho: pair.rho,
   130	    pValue: pair.pValue,
   131	    coherent: pair.coherent ?? false,
   132	    determinate: pair.determinate ?? false,
   133	    targetAnalysisRunId: pair.targetAnalysisRunId,
   134	    targetCompanionRunId: pair.targetCompanionRunId,
   135	    primaryConditionIds: pair.primaryConditionIds,
   136	    companionConditionIds: pair.companionConditionIds,
   137	    perCondition: pair.perCondition.map((condition) => ({
   138	      netPressureRank: condition.netPressureRank,
   139	      winRate: condition.winRate,
   140	      matches: condition.matches,
   141	      trials: condition.trials,
   142	      scenarioId: condition.scenarioId,
   143	    })),
   144	  }));
   145	
   146	  const determinatePairs = perPair.filter((pair) => pair.determinate).length;
   147	  const coherentPairs = perPair.filter((pair) => pair.determinate && pair.coherent).length;
   148	  const indeterminatePairs = perPair.filter((pair) => !pair.determinate).length;
   149	
   150	  return {
   151	    value: determinatePairs === 0 ? 0 : coherentPairs / determinatePairs,
   152	    coherentPairs,
   153	    determinatePairs,
   154	    indeterminatePairs,
   155	    perPair,
   156	  };
   157	}

   177	    decisionModelV2: { canonical: decisionModelV2.canonical },

     1	import type { Transcript } from '../api/operations/runs';
     2	
     3	export type CanonicalConditionSummary = {
     4	  strongly: number;
     5	  somewhat: number;
     6	  neutral: number;
     7	  opponentSomewhat: number;
     8	  opponentStrongly: number;
     9	  unknownCount: number;
    10	  totalTrials: number;
    11	  netScore: number | null;
    12	  direction: 'self' | 'opponent' | 'neutral';
    13	  hasData: boolean;
    14	};
    15	
    16	export type CanonicalConditionCounts = {
    17	  strongly: number;
    18	  somewhat: number;
    19	  neutral: number;
    20	  opponentSomewhat: number;
    21	  opponentStrongly: number;
    22	};
    23	
    24	export type CanonicalTranscriptIndex = Map<string, Map<string, Transcript[]>>;
    25	
    26	type CanonicalBucket = 'strongly' | 'somewhat' | 'neutral' | 'opponentSomewhat' | 'opponentStrongly';
    27	type CanonicalConditionTally = CanonicalConditionCounts & { unknownCount: number };
    28	
    29	function getCanonicalBucket(transcript: Transcript): CanonicalBucket | null {
    30	  const canonical = transcript.decisionModelV2?.canonical;
    31	  if (!canonical) {
    32	    return null;
    33	  }
    34	
    35	  if (canonical.strength === 'neutral') {
    36	    return 'neutral';
    37	  }
    38	
    39	  const { favoredValueKey, opposedValueKey, strength } = canonical;
    40	  if (favoredValueKey == null || opposedValueKey == null) {
    41	    return null;
    42	  }
    43	
    44	  // Alphabetically-first value is the canonical "first" (blue) side; second is
    45	  // the "opponent" (orange) side.  This is stable across both runs in a paired
    46	  // batch, unlike canonical.direction which is position-based (valueA/valueB)
    47	  // and flips between companion runs.
    48	  const isFirst = favoredValueKey.localeCompare(opposedValueKey) < 0;
    49	
    50	  if (isFirst && strength === 'strong') return 'strongly';
    51	  if (isFirst && strength === 'lean') return 'somewhat';
    52	  if (!isFirst && strength === 'lean') return 'opponentSomewhat';
    53	  if (!isFirst && strength === 'strong') return 'opponentStrongly';
    54	
    55	  return null;
    56	}
    57	
    58	export function buildCanonicalTranscriptIndex(transcripts: Transcript[] | null | undefined): CanonicalTranscriptIndex {
    59	  const index = new Map<string, Map<string, Transcript[]>>();
    60	
    61	  for (const transcript of transcripts ?? []) {
    62	    if (!transcript.scenarioId) {
    63	      continue;
    64	    }
    65	
    66	    const modelId = transcript.modelId;
    67	    const scenarioId = String(transcript.scenarioId);
    68	    const byScenario = index.get(modelId) ?? new Map<string, Transcript[]>();
    69	    const current = byScenario.get(scenarioId);
    70	    if (current) {
    71	      current.push(transcript);
    72	    } else {
    73	      byScenario.set(scenarioId, [transcript]);
    74	    }
    75	    index.set(modelId, byScenario);
    76	  }
    77	
    78	  return index;
    79	}
    80	
    81	export function collectCanonicalConditionTranscripts(
    82	  transcriptIndex: CanonicalTranscriptIndex,
    83	  modelId: string,
    84	  scenarioIds: string[],
    85	): Transcript[] {
    86	  const byScenario = transcriptIndex.get(modelId);
    87	  if (!byScenario) {
    88	    return [];
    89	  }
    90	
    91	  const collected: Transcript[] = [];
    92	  scenarioIds.forEach((scenarioId) => {
    93	    collected.push(...(byScenario.get(scenarioId) ?? []));
    94	  });
    95	  return collected;
    96	}
    97	
    98	export function summarizeCanonicalConditionTranscripts(
    99	  transcripts: Transcript[] | null | undefined,
   100	): CanonicalConditionSummary {
   101	  const counts: CanonicalConditionTally = {
   102	    strongly: 0,
   103	    somewhat: 0,
   104	    neutral: 0,
   105	    opponentSomewhat: 0,
   106	    opponentStrongly: 0,
   107	    unknownCount: 0,
   108	  };
   109	
   110	  for (const transcript of transcripts ?? []) {
   111	    const bucket = getCanonicalBucket(transcript);
   112	    if (!bucket) {
   113	      counts.unknownCount += 1;
   114	      continue;
   115	    }
   116	
   117	    counts[bucket] += 1;
   118	  }
   119	
   120	  return summarizeCanonicalConditionTally(counts);
   121	}
   122	
   123	export function summarizeCanonicalConditionCounts(
   124	  counts: CanonicalConditionCounts,
   125	): CanonicalConditionSummary {
   126	  return summarizeCanonicalConditionTally({
   127	    strongly: coerceNonNegativeFiniteCount(counts.strongly),
   128	    somewhat: coerceNonNegativeFiniteCount(counts.somewhat),
   129	    neutral: coerceNonNegativeFiniteCount(counts.neutral),
   130	    opponentSomewhat: coerceNonNegativeFiniteCount(counts.opponentSomewhat),
   131	    opponentStrongly: coerceNonNegativeFiniteCount(counts.opponentStrongly),
   132	    unknownCount: 0,
   133	  });
   134	}
   135	
   136	function coerceNonNegativeFiniteCount(value: unknown): number {
   137	  return typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value) && value >= 0 ? value : 0;
   138	}
   139	
   140	function summarizeCanonicalConditionTally(counts: CanonicalConditionTally): CanonicalConditionSummary {
   141	  const totalTrials =
   142	    counts.strongly
   143	    + counts.somewhat
   144	    + counts.neutral
   145	    + counts.opponentSomewhat
   146	    + counts.opponentStrongly;
   147	
   148	  if (totalTrials === 0) {
   149	    return {
   150	      ...counts,
   151	      totalTrials,
   152	      netScore: null,
   153	      direction: 'neutral',
   154	      hasData: false,
   155	    };
   156	  }
   157	
   158	  const netScore = (
   159	    (2 * counts.strongly + counts.somewhat)
   160	    - (2 * counts.opponentStrongly + counts.opponentSomewhat)
   161	  ) / totalTrials;
   162	
   163	  // Threshold is inclusive (>= 0.05) so it aligns with the one-decimal label:
   164	  // |netScore| >= 0.05 rounds to "0.1" and should render directional, not neutral.
   165	  let direction: CanonicalConditionSummary['direction'] = 'neutral';
   166	  if (netScore >= 0.05) {
   167	    direction = 'self';
   168	  } else if (netScore <= -0.05) {
   169	    direction = 'opponent';
   170	  }
   171	
   172	  return {
   173	    ...counts,
   174	    totalTrials,
   175	    netScore,
   176	    direction,
   177	    hasData: true,
   178	  };
   179	}
   180	
   181	export type ConditionCellDisplay = {
   182	  netScore: number | null;
   183	  direction: 'self' | 'opponent' | 'neutral';
   184	  hasData: boolean;
   185	  label: string;
   186	  backgroundColor: string | undefined;
   187	  textColorClass: string;
   188	};
   189	
   190	export function getConditionCellDisplay(summary: CanonicalConditionSummary): ConditionCellDisplay {
   191	  if (!summary.hasData) {
   192	    return {
   193	      netScore: null,
   194	      direction: summary.direction,
   195	      hasData: false,
   196	      label: '—',
   197	      backgroundColor: undefined,
   198	      textColorClass: 'text-gray-500',
   199	    };
   200	  }
   201	
   202	  const netScore = summary.netScore ?? 0;
   203	  const magnitude = Math.abs(netScore);
   204	  const label = magnitude.toFixed(1);
   205	
   206	  if (summary.direction === 'neutral') {
   207	    return {
   208	      netScore: summary.netScore,
   209	      direction: 'neutral',
   210	      hasData: true,
   211	      label,
   212	      backgroundColor: undefined,
   213	      textColorClass: 'text-gray-500',
   214	    };
   215	  }
   216	
   217	  // Directional fill: magnitude in [0, 2] maps linearly to opacity [0, 0.5].
   218	  const clamped = Math.min(2, Math.max(0, magnitude));
   219	  const opacity = (clamped / 2) * 0.5;
   220	  const isOpposing = summary.direction === 'opponent';
   221	  const backgroundColor = isOpposing
   222	    ? `rgba(251, 146, 60, ${opacity})`
   223	    : `rgba(59, 130, 246, ${opacity})`;
   224	  const textColorClass = isOpposing ? 'text-orange-700' : 'text-blue-700';
   225	
   226	  return {
   227	    netScore: summary.netScore,
   228	    direction: summary.direction,
   229	    hasData: true,
   230	    label,
   231	    backgroundColor,
   232	    textColorClass,
   233	  };
   234	}

     1	# Spec: Pressure Sensitivity Report
     2	
     3	**Feature slug:** pressure-sensitivity-report
     4	**Created:** 2026-04-27
     5	**Status:** draft
     6	**Path:** Feature Factory (`docs/workflow/feature-runs/pressure-sensitivity-report/`)
     7	
     8	---
     9	
    10	## Background
    11	
    12	ValueRank vignettes ramp up the **pressure** on each value using level scales (typically a 5-point scale: `negligible → minimal → moderate → substantial → full`, scored `1–5`). Each scenario has two pressure dimensions — pressure on the value being measured (own) and pressure on the opposing value (opponent). The system already records, per transcript, both **what a model chose** (binary: target value, opponent, or neutral) and **how firmly** (5 strength buckets: `strongly / somewhat / neutral / opponentSomewhat / opponentStrongly`), and combines those into a `netScore` ∈ [-2, 2] using a 2:1 weighting in `canonicalConditionSummary.ts`.
    13	
    14	There is no report today that asks: **as we vary pressure, how much does a model's response actually move?** The existing condition matrix shows the response inside one vignette. The new `Coherence` metric in `models-consistency-report` rolls pressure-response into a single binary per pair (coherent or not). Neither answers, per model, **which values move under pressure and which don't**, nor lets a researcher compare models on that question.
    15	
    16	This spec adds a new **Pressure Sensitivity** report under the `Models` tab. Sensitivity is treated as a descriptive characterization, not a pass/fail audit: a model can be sensitive or insensitive on any given value, and either is a legitimate finding to surface.
    17	
    18	---
    19	
    20	## Discovery: Assumptions Carried In
    21	
    22	Discovery was completed against the prior conversation. The user answered all key decisions; the following assumptions are carried in:
    23	
    24	1. **Sensitivity definition.** For each `(model, value pair)`, sensitivity is the **range of response across the 2D pressure grid** (own level × opponent level). Reported as three separate metrics, not collapsed:
    25	   - **Direction Δ** — change in win rate from low-pressure cells to high-pressure cells
    26	   - **Conviction Δ** — change in average decision strength among picks from low to high pressure
    27	   - **netScore Δ** — combined view using the existing 2:1 strongly/somewhat formula
    28	2. **Minimum N per cell = 3.** Cells below threshold are hidden or visually distinguished as low-data; they are not used in the Δ computation.
    29	3. **Layout: one model at a time.** A model selector switches the per-model detail view. No side-by-side comparison view in v1.
    30	4. **Location.** New top-level page under the existing `Models` nav link.
    31	5. **Reuse `getLevelNormalizationMap`** from `cloud/apps/api/src/graphql/queries/scenarios-utils.ts` to map scenario dimension labels back to integer level scores 1–5.
    32	6. **Skip `options[]` alternative phrasing handling.** Verified via two production definition samples and the `LevelPresetVersion` schema (single `l1`–`l5` label per level): production data does not use alternative phrasings.
    33	7. **Reuse existing strength buckets and netScore formula** from `canonicalConditionSummary.ts`. No new weighting.
    34	8. **Ceiling/floor cases are flagged, not excluded.** Flag based on baseline win rate at the lowest own-pressure level; surface via a badge so readers can distinguish "model is firm" from "no room to move".
    35	9. **New aggregation pivot.** Across vignettes for the same value pair, indexed by integer pressure level scores 1–5 on both own and opponent dimensions. The existing condition aggregation is per-vignette only.
    36	10. **No statistical machinery.** No confidence intervals, variance decomposition, multiple-comparison correction, or hypothesis testing. This is a descriptive analysis, not a research instrument.
    37	11. **No level recalibration.** Acknowledged as a measurement limitation in the on-page limitations panel.
    38	12. **No sycophancy or instruction-following confound detection.** Acknowledged in the limitations panel.
    39	
    40	---
    41	
    42	## Product Goal
    43	
    44	The `Pressure Sensitivity` report should let a researcher quickly answer:
    45	
    46	1. **Which models are most sensitive to pressure overall?** (cross-model context)
    47	2. **For a given model, which values move under pressure and which don't?** (per-model, per-value)
    48	3. **Is sensitivity a model-level trait, or is it value-specific?** (visual, via a model × value heat map)
    49	4. **Is the pressure manipulation working as designed?** (directional sanity check)
    50	
    51	All four should be answerable in under 60 seconds of scan time. Drill-down on any cell should expose the underlying counts and the formula that produced the displayed number.
    52	
    53	---
    54	
    55	## User Stories
    56	
    57	### US-1 — See cross-model sensitivity at a glance (P1)
    58	
    59	As a researcher, I want a ranked summary at the top of the page showing every model's aggregate sensitivity, so I can see how the field distributes from "most movable" to "most firm".
    60	
    61	**Why this priority:** This is the headline finding for question 1.
    62	
    63	**Independent test:** Open the report; a ranked table renders with one row per model, ordered by aggregate sensitivity, with per-value spread visible inline.
    64	
    65	**Acceptance scenarios:**
    66	
    67	1. **Given** I open `Models → Pressure Sensitivity`, **when** the page loads, **then** I see a ranked table at the top with one row per model, ordered by aggregate sensitivity (descending).
    68	2. **Given** the summary table is visible, **when** I look at any row, **then** I see a small inline distribution (sparkline or stacked bar) showing the spread of per-value sensitivity scores, so I can tell whether the aggregate reflects consistent behavior or averages over wild variation.
    69	3. **Given** a model lacks sufficient coverage to compute aggregate sensitivity, **when** the table renders, **then** that model is shown in an "Insufficient coverage" footer with a specific reason, not silently dropped.
    70	
    71	### US-2 — Drill into a single model's per-value detail (P1)
    72	
    73	As a researcher who sees a model in a surprising position on the cross-model summary, I want to select that model and see its per-value sensitivity broken out, so I can answer question 2 (which values move and which don't).
    74	
    75	**Why this priority:** This view answers the per-model question and is the diagnosis layer behind US-1.
    76	
    77	**Acceptance scenarios:**
    78	
    79	1. **Given** I select a model, **when** the per-model detail loads, **then** I see a table with one row per value pair tested for that model.
    80	2. **Given** the per-value table is visible, **when** I look at any row, **then** I see columns for: value pair, **Direction Δ** (win rate change), **Conviction Δ** (strength change), **netScore Δ** (combined), **baseline win rate** at lowest own pressure, **ceiling/floor flag**, and **N transcripts** behind the row.
    81	3. **Given** a value pair has fewer than 3 transcripts in any cell of its 2D grid, **when** the row renders, **then** the affected metric shows a low-data marker rather than a number.
    82	4. **Given** a value pair's baseline win rate is at or near 0.0 or 1.0, **when** the row renders, **then** the ceiling/floor flag is set so a reader can distinguish "model is firm" from "no room to move".
    83	5. **Given** I click a row, **when** the per-pair drill-down opens, **then** I see the **2D pressure heat map** (own level × opponent level) for that pair with the underlying counts per cell.
    84	
    85	### US-3 — Answer "is sensitivity a model trait or value-specific?" visually (P1)
    86	
    87	As a researcher who wants to characterize a model, I want a model × value heat map of sensitivity scores, so I can see whether the same model is sensitive across many values or only certain ones.
    88	
    89	**Why this priority:** This is the third of the four core questions and only one chart answers it.
    90	
    91	**Acceptance scenarios:**
    92	
    93	1. **Given** I open the report, **when** I scroll past the cross-model summary, **then** I see a heat map with one row per model and one column per value pair, cells colored by sensitivity score.
    94	2. **Given** the heat map is visible, **when** a model's row is uniformly dark or uniformly light, **then** the visual reads as "trait" (consistent across values).
    95	3. **Given** the heat map is visible, **when** a model's row varies wildly across columns, **then** the visual reads as "value-specific" (sensitive on some values, not others).
    96	4. **Given** a (model, value) cell has insufficient data, **when** the cell renders, **then** it is greyed and labeled, not zero-colored.
    97	
    98	### US-4 — Sanity-check that pressure points the right way (P1)
    99	
   100	As a researcher, I want to know whether the pressure manipulation is doing what it claims to do — does increasing own pressure on a value actually increase that value's win rate?
   101	
   102	**Why this priority:** This is the AAPOR-style validity check for the whole report. If pressure is not directional in many cases, the rest of the report becomes hard to interpret, so the check needs to be visible.
   103	
   104	**Acceptance scenarios:**
   105	
   106	1. **Given** the report is open, **when** I scroll to the bottom, **then** I see a **Directional Sanity Check** panel reporting the percentage of (model, value pair) combinations where increased own pressure produced increased win rate.
   107	2. **Given** the panel is visible, **when** I expand it, **then** I see the breakdown by direction: % positive (expected direction), % flat, % negative (against the expected direction).
   108	3. **Given** the panel is visible, **when** I click a count, **then** I see the list of (model, value pair) combinations contributing to that count.
   109	
   110	### US-5 — See math behind every number (P2)
   111	
   112	As a researcher who will cite this report, I want the formulas and intermediate counts available on the page itself, not buried in a methods doc.
   113	
   114	**Acceptance scenarios:**
   115	
   116	1. **Given** I hover or click any column header on the per-model detail table, **when** the info opens, **then** I see a plain-English definition and the formula used to compute that metric.
   117	2. **Given** I hover any displayed Δ value, **when** the tooltip opens, **then** I see the high-pressure cell summary, the low-pressure cell summary, and the subtraction that produced the Δ.
   118	3. **Given** I click a cell of the 2D heat map, **when** the panel opens, **then** I see N transcripts, win rate, conviction breakdown, and netScore for that cell.
   119	
   120	### US-6 — Limitations are surfaced on-page (P2)
   121	
   122	As a researcher, I want known caveats about this measurement to be visible on the report, not buried in documentation.
   123	
   124	**Acceptance scenarios:**
   125	
   126	1. **Given** the report is open, **when** I scroll past the directional sanity check, **then** I see a **Limitations** panel.
   127	2. **Given** the panel is visible, **when** I read it, **then** it covers: cross-vignette level calibration is assumed not validated; LLM strongly/somewhat is a self-report not a calibrated confidence scale; sycophancy and instruction-following can mimic sensitivity; cells with N < 3 are excluded.
   128	
   129	### US-7 — Empty and partial-coverage states are explicit (P2)
   130	
   131	As a user opening the report before coverage is sufficient, I want clear messaging about what is or isn't measurable.
   132	
   133	**Acceptance scenarios:**
   134	
   135	1. **Given** no models have any (own, opponent) cell with N ≥ 3, **when** the report loads, **then** an empty state explains the coverage requirement.
   136	2. **Given** some models have coverage and some don't, **when** the report loads, **then** the summary table shows the covered models and the footer lists the rest with reasons.
   137	3. **Given** a per-model row has only one or two value pairs at sufficient N, **when** the row renders, **then** a coverage warning badge indicates the aggregate is based on thin data.
   138	
   139	---
   140	
   141	## Edge Cases
   142	
   143	- **All cells in a 2D grid have N < 3 for a value pair:** the row is shown with a "low coverage" marker; Δ values are not computed.
   144	- **Only the diagonal cells are populated:** the band-coverage rule in FR-021 governs; if neither the low band (own ≤ 2) nor the high band (own ≥ 4) has a qualifying cell, Δ is undefined for that pair. No diagonal-endpoint fallback.
   145	- **Baseline win rate at lowest own pressure is at 0.0 or 1.0:** flag ceiling/floor; show the Δ values but display the flag prominently so readers do not over-interpret a small or zero Δ.
   146	- **Direction Δ is negative (pressure backfires):** show the value with the sign and a small visual flag; this case feeds the Directional Sanity Check.
   147	- **Conviction Δ is undefined** (no picks in either the low or high band): show "—" and exclude from aggregate.
   148	- **A vignette uses a non-standard level scale** (e.g., custom labels not in the normalized level map, or scores outside 1–5): drop those vignettes per FR-018 with the specific reason (c) or (d); do not fall back to raw labels and do not crash.
   149	- **A scenario dimension value contains whitespace or formatted-number variants** (e.g. `" moderate"`, `"1.0"`, `"01"`): the new aggregation MUST trim whitespace and apply the same numeric-string normalization used in `scenario-metadata.ts` before lookup. Variants that still fail lookup are excluded under FR-018 (d), not assigned to phantom buckets.
   150	- **A Definition has duplicate labels or duplicate scores across its `levels[]` array:** reject the Definition under FR-018 (e) and count the exclusion. Map-based normalization would otherwise silently overwrite earlier entries.
   151	- **A model has runs across only one own-pressure level for a value pair:** Direction and Conviction Δ are undefined for that pair; show "—".
   152	- **Definition uses legacy `values[]` (single dimension) rather than `dimensions[].levels[]`:** exclude from this report; surface count in excluded footer. Sensitivity requires two pressure dimensions.
   153	- **Two models tested on disjoint sets of value pairs:** the cross-model summary aggregate is computed only over pairs each model actually has at sufficient N; show coverage count alongside aggregate so readers can see the denominator differs.
   154	
   155	---
   156	
   157	## Functional Requirements
   158	
   159	- **FR-001:** System MUST render a Pressure Sensitivity report at a new route under the `Models` tab (exact route TBD in plan phase).
   160	- **FR-002:** System MUST aggregate transcripts by `(model, value pair, own_level_score, opponent_level_score)`, where level scores are integers in 1–5 derived from each vignette's parent Definition. Scenario dimension values MUST be extracted via `normalizeScenarioAnalysisMetadata` from `cloud/apps/api/src/services/analysis/scenario-metadata.ts` so `content.dimensions`, `content.dimension_values`, and mixed content are handled uniformly. Label-to-score mapping operates on each Definition's `dimensions[].levels[]` array (NOT on `content` directly): `getLevelNormalizationMap` accepts a `DefinitionDimension` and reads its `levels[]`/`values[]` to build a normalized score map. Two known gaps in that helper are addressed in this spec: (i) it uses raw, untrimmed string keys, so the new aggregation MUST trim/normalize scenario-side dimension values via the same `toComparableNumber`-style logic from `scenario-metadata.ts` BEFORE map lookup; (ii) it has no collision detection — see FR-002a.
   161	- **FR-002a:** Level-label normalization MUST detect map-collision risk in the parent Definition. A Definition's `levels[]` array is in collision if (i) any two levels share the same label after trim/normalization, (ii) any two levels share the same score, or (iii) any one level's label equals any other level's score after string coercion. The new aggregation MUST run a pre-pass over each Definition's raw `dimensions[].levels[]` array (not the post-normalization map) to detect collisions and reject the entire Definition under FR-018 reason (e). Both label and score may be used as map keys for normal lookup, because rejection-on-collision guarantees no key conflict survives. This rule replaces the alternative "label-only keying" option for clarity.
   162	- **FR-002b:** All scenario-dimension extraction and label normalization MUST go through a **single canonical pipeline** built by importing existing helpers — not re-implementing them. Specifically: the new aggregation MUST (i) read scenario dimension values via `normalizeScenarioAnalysisMetadata` from `cloud/apps/api/src/services/analysis/scenario-metadata.ts` so `dimensions`, `dimension_values`, and mixed content are handled uniformly, and (ii) map labels to scores via the same Definition-level lookup, applying `toComparableNumber`-style trimming and numeric-equivalence handling consistent with `scenario-metadata.ts`. Re-implementing extraction or normalization in the new resolver is out of bounds; the resolver wires existing helpers together and adds aggregation logic.
   163	- **FR-003:** For each cell in the (own × opponent) grid, system MUST compute the following four cell-level metrics with explicit formulas:
   164	  (a) **N** = total transcripts in the cell (no exclusion of strength buckets);
   165	  (b) **Win rate** = `prioritized / (prioritized + deprioritized + neutral)`, where the prioritized/deprioritized/neutral classification per transcript comes from `buildValueOutcomes` / `buildCanonicalValueOutcomes` in `cloud/apps/api/src/services/analysis/aggregate/aggregate-helpers.ts` (which already handles orientation flip on the response score per FR-019). The denominator uses the same convention surfaced in the `ModelValueDetailDrawer.tsx` tooltip text: this is the fraction of trials where the target value won, with `neutral` in the denominator and `unscored` (refusal/unknown per FR-023) excluded entirely. The win rate metric is canonical for the Models UI but is not pre-computed in `canonicalConditionSummary.ts`; that file computes `netScore` and bucket counts. Implementation MUST derive win rate from those counts. Tooltips (per FR-015) MUST state explicitly that this is "wins over scored trials," excluding refusals/unknowns;
   166	  (c) **Conviction** = `(2 × strongly + somewhat) / (strongly + somewhat)` when `(strongly + somewhat) ≥ 1`, otherwise undefined for the cell. This is the mean strength **among target-value picks only** (denominator excludes neutral and opponent picks); it is NOT the netScore formula. Cells with zero target-value picks contribute nothing to the conviction band mean.
   167	  (d) **netScore** = existing formula `(2 × strongly + somewhat − 2 × opponentStrongly − opponentSomewhat) / totalTrials` from `canonicalConditionSummary.ts`, unchanged.
   168	- **FR-004:** Cells with N < 3 MUST be marked as low-data and excluded from Δ computation. The threshold is configurable in code (constant, not a user-facing filter in v1).
   169	- **FR-005:** For each `(model, value pair)`, system MUST compute three Δ metrics across the populated cells of the (own × opponent) grid:
   170	  (a) **Direction Δ** = high-band mean win rate − low-band mean win rate, where the high band is cells with own_level_score ≥ 4 and the low band is cells with own_level_score ≤ 2; if either band is empty, Δ is undefined;
   171	  (b) **Conviction Δ** = same band logic applied to the conviction metric;
   172	  (c) **netScore Δ** = same band logic applied to the netScore metric.
   173	- **FR-006:** For each `(model, value pair)`, system MUST compute a **baseline win rate** = mean win rate across cells with own_level_score = 1 that meet the N ≥ 3 threshold (per FR-004). If no level-1 cell meets the threshold, fall back to the lowest populated level whose cell meets N ≥ 3. If no own-pressure level has any qualifying cell, the baseline is undefined and ceiling/floor flags are not applied.
   174	- **FR-007:** A pair is flagged **ceiling** if baseline win rate ≥ 0.9, **floor** if baseline win rate ≤ 0.1, otherwise no flag. Threshold values are constants in code.
   175	- **FR-008:** For each model, system MUST compute an **aggregate sensitivity** = mean of the absolute netScore Δ across the model's value pairs that have a defined Δ.
   176	- **FR-009:** System MUST render the cross-model summary table with one row per model and columns: model name, provider, aggregate sensitivity, inline per-value spread visualization, count of value pairs measured, count of value pairs excluded.
   177	- **FR-010:** System MUST render a per-model detail view with one row per value pair and columns: value pair, Direction Δ, Conviction Δ, netScore Δ, baseline win rate, ceiling/floor flag, N transcripts.
   178	- **FR-011:** System MUST render a 2D pressure heat map (own × opponent) per value pair on demand (e.g., row click), showing the chosen metric per cell with the underlying N visible on hover.
   179	- **FR-012:** System MUST render a model × value heat map at the page level showing aggregate sensitivity per (model, value pair) cell, with low-coverage cells visually distinguished.
   180	- **FR-013:** System MUST render a **Directional Sanity Check** panel at the bottom of the page reporting the percentage of (model, value pair) combinations where Direction Δ is positive (expected), zero/flat (|Δ| < 0.02), or negative (against expectation). The denominator is the count of (model, value pair) combinations with a **defined** Direction Δ — pairs with undefined Δ (insufficient band coverage per FR-021) are reported as a separate "unmeasurable" count next to the panel, not in the percentage. Click-through MUST surface the underlying list filtered by direction or unmeasurable.
   181	- **FR-014:** System MUST render a **Limitations** panel that surfaces, prominently and on the page itself (not in tooltips):
   182	  (a) **Cross-vignette level calibration is not validated** — comparing sensitivity scores across different value pairs is suspect because "pressure level 4" in one vignette may not represent the same intensity as "pressure level 4" in another. This caveat applies directly to the cross-model summary (US-1) and the model × value heat map (US-3); both views should display the limitation badge inline near their headers.
   183	  (b) **Conviction is a model self-report**, not a calibrated confidence scale. Sycophantic models can increase stated conviction without changing decision logic, which would inflate this report's Conviction Δ.
   184	  (c) **Sycophancy and instruction-following can mimic sensitivity** — a model that mirrors prompt emphasis without actual value reasoning will look pressure-sensitive to this report.
   185	  (d) **Cells with N < 3 are excluded** — see FR-004, FR-006, FR-021.
   186	  (e) **Excluded vignettes are counted, not silenced** — see FR-018.
   187	  (f) **Banding intentionally averages across opponent pressure** — Direction Δ, Conviction Δ, and netScore Δ collapse the 2D grid to a 1D summary by mean-aggregating each band over opponent levels. This can mask interactions where a model becomes more (or less) firm specifically when opponent pressure is high. The per-pair 2D heat map (FR-011) is the only place these interactions remain visible; the panel MUST link to it.
   188	  (g) **Aggregate sensitivity is unweighted by coverage** — `mean(|netScore Δ|)` across pairs treats a model measured on three highly elastic pairs as more sensitive than a model measured on twelve modestly elastic pairs. The cross-model summary (FR-009) shows the pair count alongside the aggregate; the limitations panel MUST also state this explicitly so readers do not over-rank thin-coverage models.
   189	- **FR-015:** System MUST surface every displayed metric's plain-English definition and formula on the page (tooltip or inline expander acceptable). Every Δ MUST be traceable on hover or click to the band means and counts that produced it.
   190	- **FR-016:** System MUST handle the empty and partial-coverage states described in US-7: pipeline-pending empty state, per-model insufficient-coverage footer, per-row low-coverage warning badge.
   191	- **FR-017:** System MUST NOT modify the existing condition analysis resolvers, `ConditionMatrix`, or the `Coherence` metric introduced by `models-consistency-report`. The new aggregation is additive.
   192	- **FR-017a:** **Run-scope filter.** The new aggregation MUST follow the same run-scope boundary used by `models-consistency.ts`: include only runs with `status = 'COMPLETED'`, `deletedAt IS NULL`, and the `AGGREGATE` analysis tag (or the equivalent canonical filter the consistency report uses). Any other run state MUST be excluded silently from the aggregation; no transcripts from other runs may enter the (own × opponent) grid.
   193	- **FR-018:** System MUST exclude vignettes that cannot be aggregated and account for the exclusion reason via a **raw-definition validation pass** that runs against each Definition's raw `content` BEFORE label normalization erases the signal. The pass produces a per-Definition `exclusion_reason` value drawn from this enumeration (mutually exclusive; first match wins, evaluated top-to-bottom):
   194	  (a) **legacy single-dimension content** — definition uses `values[]` with no `dimensions[].levels[]` structure (sensitivity requires two pressure dimensions);
   195	  (b) **missing or empty `levels[]`** — definition has `dimensions[]` but no usable level scores;
   196	  (c) **out-of-range or non-integer scores** — definition has scores outside 1–5 or non-integer scores (e.g. 0, 6, 1.5);
   197	  (d) **unrecognized scenario labels** — scenario dimension value does not exact-match any known label/score in the parent definition's normalized level map (no fallback to raw label; phantom buckets are not allowed);
   198	  (e) **map collision** — see FR-002a;
   199	  (f) **insufficient cell coverage** — every (own × opponent) cell has N < 3 (this reason is decided after aggregation, not in the raw-definition pass; the pass marks the Definition as eligible and the aggregator decides (f) post-bucket).
   200	  Distinguishing (a) from (b) requires inspecting both `dimensions` and `values` content paths; using only `getLevelNormalizationMap`'s empty-map signal is not sufficient. The validation pass MUST persist the per-Definition exclusion reason alongside the aggregated counts so the "excluded" footer can later cite it without re-deriving from sanitized records.
   201	- **FR-019:** System MUST handle `scenario.orientationFlipped = true` correctly. The `6 - score` rule in `cloud/apps/api/src/services/analysis/aggregate/aggregate-helpers.ts` operates on the model's **response score** (the 1-5 response scale that maps to prioritized/deprioritized/neutral via `buildValueOutcomes`), NOT on input pressure level scores. The new aggregation MUST therefore:
   202	  (a) Use the existing `buildValueOutcomes` helper (or its canonical equivalent) so the prioritized/deprioritized/neutral classification is computed correctly for flipped scenarios — never re-implement this logic with raw response scores;
   203	  (b) Determine the dimension-to-value mapping from `Definition.content.components.value_first.token` / `value_second.token`, which is stable across flipped and non-flipped scenarios. In other words: `orientationFlipped` does NOT affect which scenario dimension corresponds to which value's pressure level. It only affects how the response score is interpreted as a vote.
   204	  (c) Apply NO transformation (`6 - score` or otherwise) to pressure level scores derived from scenario dimension values. Pressure level scores enter the (own × opponent) grid as-is once mapped via FR-002b.
   205	- **FR-020:** System MUST validate that level scores from each Definition are integers in 1–5 before using them. Vignettes whose Definition declares scores outside this range MUST be excluded under FR-018 reason (c). The validation MUST run once per Definition during aggregation, not per transcript.
   206	- **FR-021:** Direction Δ, Conviction Δ, and netScore Δ MUST require **at least one populated cell in each band** (low band: own_level_score ≤ 2, qualifying per N ≥ 3; high band: own_level_score ≥ 4, qualifying per N ≥ 3). If either band has zero qualifying cells, Δ is undefined; do not fall back to diagonal endpoints or impute. The "diagonal-only populated grid" edge case is resolved by this rule: if a band has no qualifying cell, Δ is undefined regardless of what the other cells contain.
   207	- **FR-022:** **Pooling rule.** Each transcript contributes to N exactly once via the same pooling boundary used by the rest of the Models tab (per `ModelValueDetailDrawer.tsx`): observations are pooled at the `(model, scenario)` level after the AGGREGATE analysis stage, so that re-running the same scenario for the same model does not inflate N. The new aggregation MUST therefore source its inputs from the AGGREGATE analysis records (filtered per FR-017a) rather than raw transcript rows; if a needed value is not exposed in AGGREGATE, the resolver MUST pool at `(model, scenario)` before counting toward N. Pooling MUST be applied before the N ≥ 3 threshold is evaluated.
   208	- **FR-023:** **Refusal and unknown direction handling.** Transcripts whose canonical direction is `refusal` or `unknown` (per `aggregate-helpers.ts` `buildCanonicalValueOutcomes` and `canonicalConditionSummary.ts` `unknownCount`) MUST be excluded from N, win rate, conviction, and netScore for the cell — they are not "neutral". The aggregation MUST track an `unscored_count` per cell (refusals + unknowns) and surface the total per `(model, value pair)` in the per-model detail view (a small inline indicator next to N), so a high refusal rate is not silently treated as low coverage.
   209	- **FR-024:** **Value pair canonicalization.** Value pair identity is the canonical key `[token_a, token_b].sort().join('::')` derived from `Definition.content.components.value_first.token` and `value_second.token`. Mirrored definitions (e.g., `Power -> Achievement` and `Achievement -> Power`) collapse to the same value pair bucket. Within a value pair, "own" is the user-selected analysis target; "opponent" is the other token. The own/opponent dimension lookup is then resolved per FR-002b — for each scenario the resolver consults the Definition to determine which `dimensions[]` entry corresponds to the user-selected own value, regardless of whether the Definition lists own first or second. Value pairs that cannot be canonicalized (missing or non-string tokens) are excluded under FR-018 with a new reason category (g) **missing value-pair tokens**.
   210	
   211	---
   212	
   213	## Success Criteria
   214	
   215	- **SC-001:** A researcher reading the report for the first time can answer the four product-goal questions in under 60 seconds of scan time.
   216	- **SC-002:** Every numeric value on the page is traceable to a formula and to raw counts in three or fewer interactions.
   217	- **SC-003:** No existing Models-tab test passes become failures; the existing `cross-domain Stability` and `Consistency` views are unchanged.
   218	- **SC-004:** The report renders in under 2 seconds with the full current model roster once analysis data is in cache.
   219	- **SC-005:** A user opening the report before coverage exists sees the empty state, not a broken chart or a chart of zeroes.
   220	- **SC-006:** On-page copy for every metric reads at high-school level (Flesch-Kincaid grade 10 or below) and the exact formula appears on the page.
   221	- **SC-007:** The Directional Sanity Check reports a number; if it shows < 70% positive direction, the report still renders without misleading the reader (limitations panel and on-page note explain).
   222	
   223	---
   224	
   225	## Non-Goals
   226	
   227	- Adding or changing models, vignettes, value pairs, or domains.
   228	- Statistical machinery: confidence intervals, variance decomposition, multiple-comparison correction, formal hypothesis testing.
   229	- Recalibrating level labels across vignettes.
   230	- Detecting or mitigating sycophancy or instruction-following confounds.
   231	- Handling `options[]` alternative phrasings on `DimensionLevel` (unused in production data).
   232	- Curve fitting, slope regression, or any analysis that assumes monotonic response to pressure.
   233	- Multi-model side-by-side comparison view (deferred).
   234	- Re-implementing or replacing the `Coherence` metric from `models-consistency-report`. The two reports are additive: `Coherence` is a single binary per pair; this report decomposes the response.
   235	- Modifying or embedding `ConditionMatrix` or `PairedStabilityView`. Optional links to those existing pages are acceptable, not embedding.
   236	
   237	---
   238	
   239	## Open Questions
   240	
   241	*(Do not block spec checkpoint; resolved in plan phase.)*
   242	
   243	1. **Exact route** — `Models → Pressure Sensitivity` as a tab inside Models, or a top-level child route. To be decided alongside the existing Models nav structure.
   244	2. **Aggregate sensitivity formula** — current spec uses `mean(|netScore Δ|)` across pairs. Alternatives: median (less sensitive to one outlier pair), or a weighted mean by N. Plan phase to confirm.
   245	3. ~~High/low band thresholds~~ — **decided**: own_level_score ≥ 4 (high band), ≤ 2 (low band), level 3 intentionally excluded from the band split to compare extremes. FR-005 is canonical; this open question is closed.
   246	4. **2D heat map: which metric drives the cell color by default?** — netScore Δ is the headline, but per-cell display may default to win rate. Plan phase to confirm the default and any toggle.
   247	5. **Inline per-value spread visualization** — sparkline, mini stacked bar, or bullet chart. Plan phase to choose based on the components already in the codebase.
   248	
   249	---
   250	
   251	## Dependencies
   252	
   253	- **`scenario-metadata.ts`** in `cloud/apps/api/src/services/analysis/` continues to be the canonical extractor for scenario dimension values across `dimensions`, `dimension_values`, and mixed content paths. The new aggregation reuses this rather than re-implementing extraction.
   254	- **`getLevelNormalizationMap`** in `cloud/apps/api/src/graphql/queries/scenarios-utils.ts` is consulted for label-to-score mapping per Definition. Known gaps documented in this spec: it only reads `content.dimensions`, uses exact-match keys, and does not guard against label-vs-score collisions or out-of-range scores. The new aggregation MUST work around these gaps per FR-002, FR-002a, FR-018, FR-020.
   255	- **Orientation flip** convention in `cloud/apps/api/src/services/analysis/aggregate/aggregate-helpers.ts` (`6 - score` rule when `orientationFlipped = true`) and the threading pattern in `cloud/apps/api/src/graphql/queries/models-consistency.ts` are reused per FR-019.
   256	- **Decision strength buckets** (`strongly`, `somewhat`, `neutral`, `opponentSomewhat`, `opponentStrongly`) and the `netScore` formula in `canonicalConditionSummary.ts` are reused as the canonical decision representation. Win-rate denominator follows the same canonical formula (`prioritized / total` including neutral).
   257	- **Definition content schema** continues to expose `dimensions[].levels[].score` for vignettes that follow the current pattern. Legacy `values[]` and `dimension_values` paths are recognized for exclusion accounting (FR-018) but do not contribute to the aggregation in v1.
   258	- **GraphQL schema** for transcripts/scenarios continues to expose decision strength, scenario dimension values, and `scenario.orientationFlipped`.
   259	
   260	---

     9	import { buildCanonicalValueOutcomes } from './aggregate-helpers.js';
    10	
    11	type TranscriptRecordForBuilder = {
    12	  id: string;
    13	  runId: string;
    14	  sampleIndex: number;
    15	  modelId: string;
    16	  scenarioId: string | null;
    17	  decisionMetadata: unknown;
    18	  definitionSnapshot: unknown;
    19	  scenario: {
    20	    id: string;
    21	    name: string;
    22	    deletedAt: Date | null;
    23	    orientationFlipped: boolean;
    24	    content: unknown;
    25	  } | null;
    26	};
    27	
    28	export function buildAggregateWorkerTranscripts(
    29	  transcripts: TranscriptRecordForBuilder[],
    30	  valueA: string | null,
    31	  valueB: string | null,
    32	): AggregateWorkerTranscript[] {
    33	  return transcripts.map((transcript) => {
    34	    const normalizedScenarioMetadata = normalizeScenarioAnalysisMetadata(transcript.scenario?.content ?? null);
    35	    const dimensions = buildScenarioAnalysisDimensionRecord(normalizedScenarioMetadata);
    36	    const orientationFlipped = transcript.scenario?.orientationFlipped ?? false;
    37	    const resolved = resolveTranscriptDecisionModel({
    38	      decisionMetadata: transcript.decisionMetadata,
    39	      definitionSnapshot: transcript.definitionSnapshot,
    40	      orientationFlipped,
    41	    });
    42	    const values = buildCanonicalValueOutcomes(
    43	      resolved.canonical.direction,
    44	      valueA,
    45	      valueB,
    46	    );
    47	
    48	    return {
    49	      id: transcript.id,
    50	      runId: transcript.runId,
    51	      modelId: transcript.modelId,
    52	      scenarioId: transcript.scenarioId!,
    53	      sampleIndex: transcript.sampleIndex,
    54	      orientationFlipped,
    55	      summary: values != null ? { values } : {},
    56	      decisionModelV2: resolved.canonical.direction !== 'unknown'
    57	        ? { canonical: { direction: resolved.canonical.direction, strength: resolved.canonical.strength } }
    58	        : null,
    59	      scenario: {
    60	        name: transcript.scenario?.name ?? transcript.scenarioId ?? '',
    61	        dimensions,
    62	      },
    63	    };
    64	  });
    65	}

   322	    .slice(0, 20);
   323	
   324	  const dimensionsMap: Record<string, Record<string, number | string>> = {};
   325	  scenarios.forEach((scenario) => {
   326	    const normalized = normalizeScenarioAnalysisMetadata(scenario.content);
   327	    if (normalized) {
   328	      dimensionsMap[scenario.id] = normalized.groupingDimensions;
   329	    }
   330	  });
   331	  mergedVizData.scenarioDimensions = dimensionsMap;
   332	
   333	  const varianceAnalysis = computeVarianceAnalysis(transcripts, scenarios);
   334	  const normalizedArtifacts = normalizeAnalysisArtifacts({
   335	    visualizationData: mergedVizData,
   336	    varianceAnalysis,
   337	    scenarios: scenarios.map((scenario) => ({
   338	      id: scenario.id,
   339	      name: scenario.name,
   340	      content: scenario.content,
   341	    })),
   342	  });
   343	
   344	  const normalizedVisualizationData = isAggregatedVisualizationData(normalizedArtifacts.visualizationData)
   345	    ? normalizedArtifacts.visualizationData
   346	    : mergedVizData;
   347	  const normalizedVarianceAnalysis = isRunVarianceAnalysis(normalizedArtifacts.varianceAnalysis)
   348	    ? normalizedArtifacts.varianceAnalysis
   349	    : varianceAnalysis;
   350	
   351	  return {
   352	    perModel: aggregatedPerModel,
   353	    modelAgreement: template.modelAgreement,
   354	    visualizationData: normalizedVisualizationData,
   355	    mostContestedScenarios: mergedContested,
   356	    varianceAnalysis: normalizedVarianceAnalysis,
   357	    decisionStats: decisionStats as Record<string, DecisionStats>,
   358	    valueAggregateStats,
   359	  };
   360	}

     1	import { normalizeScenarioAnalysisMetadata } from './scenario-metadata.js';
     2	
     3	type ScenarioRecord = {
     4	  id: string;
     5	  name: string;
     6	  content: unknown;
     7	};
     8	
     9	type NormalizedArtifacts = {
    10	  visualizationData: Record<string, unknown> | null;
    11	  varianceAnalysis: Record<string, unknown> | null;
    12	};
    13	
    14	function isPlainObject(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
    15	  return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
    16	}
    17	
    18	function isUnknownArray(value: unknown): value is unknown[] {
    19	  return Array.isArray(value);
    20	}
    21	
    22	function toDimensionRecord(value: unknown): Record<string, number | string> | null {
    23	  if (!isPlainObject(value)) return null;
    24	  const sanitized: Record<string, number | string> = {};
    25	  for (const [key, entry] of Object.entries(value)) {
    26	    if (typeof entry !== 'number' && typeof entry !== 'string') continue;
    27	    sanitized[key] = entry;
    28	  }
    29	  return Object.keys(sanitized).length > 0 ? sanitized : null;
    30	}
    31	
    32	function resolveScenarioId(
    33	  scenarioKey: string,
    34	  knownScenarioIds: Set<string>,
    35	  scenarioNameToId: Map<string, string>
    36	): string | null {
    37	  if (knownScenarioIds.has(scenarioKey)) return scenarioKey;
    38	  return scenarioNameToId.get(scenarioKey) ?? null;
    39	}
    40	
    41	function normalizeScenarioDimensions(
    42	  rawDimensions: unknown,
    43	  scenarios: ScenarioRecord[]
    44	): Record<string, Record<string, number | string>> {
    45	  const canonical: Record<string, Record<string, number | string>> = {};
    46	  for (const scenario of scenarios) {
    47	    const normalized = normalizeScenarioAnalysisMetadata(scenario.content);
    48	    if (normalized !== null) {
    49	      canonical[scenario.id] = normalized.groupingDimensions;
    50	    }
    51	  }
    52	
    53	  if (Object.keys(canonical).length > 0) {
    54	    return canonical;
    55	  }
    56	
    57	  if (!isPlainObject(rawDimensions)) {
    58	    return {};
    59	  }
    60	
    61	  const fallback: Record<string, Record<string, number | string>> = {};
    62	  for (const [scenarioId, dims] of Object.entries(rawDimensions)) {
    63	    const validated = toDimensionRecord(dims);
    64	    if (validated !== null) {
    65	      fallback[scenarioId] = validated;
    66	    }
    67	  }
    68	  return fallback;
    69	}
    70	
    71	function normalizeModelScenarioMatrix(
    72	  rawMatrix: unknown,
    73	  knownScenarioIds: Set<string>,
    74	  scenarioNameToId: Map<string, string>
    75	): Record<string, Record<string, number>> | undefined {
    76	  if (!isPlainObject(rawMatrix)) return undefined;
    77	
    78	  const normalized: Record<string, Record<string, number>> = {};
    79	  for (const [modelId, scenarios] of Object.entries(rawMatrix)) {
    80	    if (!isPlainObject(scenarios)) continue;
    81	    const outScenarios: Record<string, number> = {};
    82	    for (const [scenarioKey, score] of Object.entries(scenarios)) {
    83	      if (typeof score !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(score)) continue;
    84	      const canonicalScenarioId = resolveScenarioId(scenarioKey, knownScenarioIds, scenarioNameToId);
    85	      if (canonicalScenarioId === null) continue;
    86	      if (scenarioKey === canonicalScenarioId || outScenarios[canonicalScenarioId] === undefined) {
    87	        outScenarios[canonicalScenarioId] = score;
    88	      }
    89	    }
    90	    normalized[modelId] = outScenarios;
    91	  }
    92	  return normalized;
    93	}
    94	
    95	function normalizeVarianceAnalysis(
    96	  rawVariance: unknown,
    97	  knownScenarioIds: Set<string>,
    98	  scenarioNameToId: Map<string, string>,
    99	  scenarioIdToName: Map<string, string>
   100	): Record<string, unknown> | null {
   101	  if (!isPlainObject(rawVariance)) return null;
   102	
   103	  const out: Record<string, unknown> = { ...rawVariance };
   104	
   105	  const perModel = out.perModel;
   106	  if (isPlainObject(perModel)) {
   107	    const normalizedPerModel: Record<string, unknown> = {};
   108	    for (const [modelId, modelStats] of Object.entries(perModel)) {
   109	      if (!isPlainObject(modelStats)) {
   110	        normalizedPerModel[modelId] = modelStats;
   111	        continue;
   112	      }
   113	      const normalizedModelStats: Record<string, unknown> = { ...modelStats };
   114	      const perScenario = modelStats.perScenario;
   115	      if (isPlainObject(perScenario)) {
   116	        const normalizedPerScenario: Record<string, unknown> = {};
   117	        for (const [scenarioKey, stats] of Object.entries(perScenario)) {
   118	          const canonicalScenarioId = resolveScenarioId(scenarioKey, knownScenarioIds, scenarioNameToId);
   119	          if (canonicalScenarioId === null) continue;
   120	          if (scenarioKey === canonicalScenarioId || normalizedPerScenario[canonicalScenarioId] === undefined) {
   121	            normalizedPerScenario[canonicalScenarioId] = stats;
   122	          }
   123	        }
   124	        normalizedModelStats.perScenario = normalizedPerScenario;
   125	      }
   126	      normalizedPerModel[modelId] = normalizedModelStats;
   127	    }
   128	    out.perModel = normalizedPerModel;
   129	  }
   130	
   131	  const normalizeScenarioList = (value: unknown): unknown => {
   132	    if (!isUnknownArray(value)) return value;
   133	    return value.map((entry: unknown) => {
   134	      if (!isPlainObject(entry)) return entry;
   135	      const rawScenarioId = typeof entry.scenarioId === 'string' ? entry.scenarioId : null;
   136	      if (rawScenarioId === null) return entry;
   137	      const canonicalScenarioId = resolveScenarioId(rawScenarioId, knownScenarioIds, scenarioNameToId);
   138	      if (canonicalScenarioId === null) return entry;
   139	      const scenarioName = scenarioIdToName.get(canonicalScenarioId);
   140	      return {
   141	        ...entry,
   142	        scenarioId: canonicalScenarioId,
   143	        ...(typeof scenarioName === 'string' && scenarioName !== '' ? { scenarioName } : {}),
   144	      };
   145	    });
   146	  };
   147	
   148	  out.mostVariableScenarios = normalizeScenarioList(out.mostVariableScenarios);
   149	  out.leastVariableScenarios = normalizeScenarioList(out.leastVariableScenarios);
   150	
   151	  return out;
   152	}
   153

   157	  scenarios: ScenarioRecord[];
   158	}): NormalizedArtifacts {
   159	  const { visualizationData, varianceAnalysis, scenarios } = params;
   160	  const scenarioNameToId = new Map<string, string>();

    48	  // Memoized to keep the object reference stable across renders.

    63	    query: LLM_MODELS_QUERY,

    65	    requestPolicy: 'cache-and-network',
    66	  });
    67	  const [selectedModelIds, setSelectedModelIds] = useState<string[]>([]);
    68	  const [selectedCell, setSelectedCell] = useState<{ modelId: string; valueKey: string } | null>(null);
    69	  const initializedModelSelection = useRef(false);
    70	
    71	  const models = useMemo(() => data?.modelsAnalysis.models ?? [], [data]);
    72	  const defaultModelIds = useMemo(
    73	    () => new Set((llmModelsData?.llmModels ?? []).filter((m) => m.isDefault).map((m) => m.modelId)),
    74	    [llmModelsData],
    75	  );
    76	  const selectedDomain = selectedDomainId != null ? domains.find((domain) => domain.id === selectedDomainId) ?? null : null;
    77	  const singleDomainActive = selectedDomainId != null;
    78	
    79	  useEffect(() => {
    80	    if (selectedDomainId == null) return;
    81	    if (domains.some((domain) => domain.id === selectedDomainId)) return;
    82	    setSelectedDomainId(null);
    83	  }, [domains, selectedDomainId]);
    84	
    85	  useEffect(() => {
    86	    if (initializedModelSelection.current) return;
    87	    if (models.length === 0) return;
    88	    if (llmModelsData == null) return;
    89	    const availableIds = models.map((model) => model.modelId);
    90	    const defaultSelection = availableIds.filter((id) => defaultModelIds.has(id));
    91	    // Fall back to all models if no defaults are configured or none match the current result set
    92	    setSelectedModelIds(defaultSelection.length > 0 ? defaultSelection : availableIds);
    93	    initializedModelSelection.current = true;
    94	  }, [models, llmModelsData, defaultModelIds]);
    95	
    96	  useEffect(() => {
    97	    if (!initializedModelSelection.current) return;
    98	    setSelectedModelIds((current) => {
    99	      if (current.length === 0) return current;
   100	      const validIds = new Set(models.map((model) => model.modelId));
   101	      const next = current.filter((modelId) => validIds.has(modelId));
   102	      return next.length === current.length ? current : next;
   103	    });
   104	  }, [models]);
   105	
   106	  // Close the drawer when its model is no longer visible (filtered out or cleared)
   107	  useEffect(() => {
   108	    if (selectedCell == null) return;
   109	    if (!selectedModelIds.includes(selectedCell.modelId)) {
   110	      setSelectedCell(null);
   111	    }
   112	  }, [selectedModelIds, selectedCell]);
   113	
   114	  const modelOptions = useMemo(() => models.map((model) => ({
   115	    value: model.modelId,
   116	    label: model.label,
   117	  })), [models]);
   118	
   119	  const domainOptions = useMemo(() => [

   122	      value: domain.id,
   123	      label: `${domain.name}`,
   124	    })),
   125	  ], [domains]);
   126

   129	    : null;
   130	  const selectedValue: ModelsAnalysisValueResult | null = useMemo(() => {
   131	    if (selectedCell == null || selectedModel == null) return null;
   132	    return selectedModel.values.find((value) => value.valueKey === selectedCell.valueKey) ?? null;
   133	  }, [selectedCell, selectedModel]);
   134	
   135	  const defaultSelection = useMemo(() => {
   136	    const availableIds = models.map((model) => model.modelId);
   137	    const defaults = availableIds.filter((id) => defaultModelIds.has(id));
   138	    return defaults.length > 0 ? defaults : availableIds;
   139	  }, [models, defaultModelIds]);
   140	
   141	  const isDefaultSelection = useMemo(() => {
   142	    if (selectedModelIds.length !== defaultSelection.length) return false;
   143	    const defaultSet = new Set(defaultSelection);
   144	    return selectedModelIds.every((id) => defaultSet.has(id));
   145	  }, [selectedModelIds, defaultSelection]);
   146	
   147	  const loading = (domainsLoading && domains.length === 0) || (fetching && data == null);
   148	  const selectedModelCount = selectedModelIds.length;
   149	
   150	  const toggleModelId = (modelId: string) => {
   151	    setSelectedModelIds((current) => (
   152	      current.includes(modelId)
   153	        ? current.filter((id) => id !== modelId)
   154	        : [...current, modelId]
   155	    ));
   156	  };
   157	
   158	  const selectAllModels = () => {
   159	    setSelectedModelIds(models.map((model) => model.modelId));
   160	  };
   161	
   162	  const clearModels = () => {
   163	    setSelectedModelIds([]);
   164	  };
   165	
   166	  const handleCellClick = (modelId: string, valueKey: string) => {
   167	    setSelectedCell({ modelId, valueKey });
   168	  };
   169	
   170	  const modelSetSummary = loading
   171	    ? 'Loading...'
   172	    : modelOptions.length === 0
   173	      ? 'No models available'
   174	      : isDefaultSelection
   175	        ? `Default — ${selectedModelCount} models selected`
   176	        : selectedModelCount === modelOptions.length
   177	          ? 'All selected'
   178	          : `${selectedModelCount} of ${modelOptions.length} selected`;
   179	
   180	  return (
   181	    <div className="space-y-6">
   182	      <div className="space-y-2">
   183	        <h1 className="text-2xl font-serif font-medium text-[#1A1A1A]">Models</h1>
   184	        <p className="text-sm text-gray-600">
   185	          Compare model preferences by value and scan whether each pattern stays steady across domains.
   186	        </p>
   187	      </div>
   188	
   189	      {(domainsError != null || error != null) && (
   190	        <ErrorMessage message={`Failed to load models analysis: ${(domainsError ?? error)?.message ?? 'Unknown error'}`} />
   191	      )}
   192	
   193	      <section className="rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-white p-4 md:p-5">
   194	        <div className="flex flex-wrap items-start gap-4">
   195	          <div className="w-48 flex-shrink-0">
   196	            <Select
   197	              label="Domain scope"
   198	              options={domainOptions}
   199	              value={selectedDomainId ?? 'all'}
   200	              onChange={(value) => setSelectedDomainId(value === 'all' ? null : value)}
   201	              placeholder="All domains"
   202	            />
   203	          </div>
   204	          {selectedDomainId != null && signatureOptions.length > 0 && (
   205	            <div className="w-48 flex-shrink-0">
   206	              <Select
   207	                label="Batch"
   208	                options={signatureOptions.map((o) => ({ value: o.signature, label: formatSignatureOptionLabel(o) }))}
   209	                value={selectedSignature}
   210	                onChange={(value) => setSelectedSignature(value)}
   211	              />
   212	            </div>
   213	          )}
   214	          <details className="flex-1 min-w-[220px]">
   215	            <summary className="cursor-pointer list-none">
   216	              <p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1">Model set</p>
   217	              <div className="inline-flex w-full items-center justify-between rounded-lg border border-gray-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm hover:border-gray-400 min-h-[44px] sm:min-h-0">
   218	                <span className={modelOptions.length === 0 && !loading ? 'text-gray-400' : ''}>
   219	                  {modelSetSummary}
   220	                </span>
   221	                <span className="ml-2 text-gray-400">▾</span>
   222	              </div>
   223	            </summary>
   224	            <div className="mt-2 space-y-3 rounded-lg border border-gray-200 bg-white p-3">
   225	              <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center justify-between gap-2">
   226	                <p className="text-xs text-gray-600">All visible models are selected by default.</p>
   227	                <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   228	                  <Button type="button" variant="secondary" size="sm" onClick={selectAllModels} disabled={loading}>
   229	                    Select all
   230	                  </Button>
   231	                  <Button type="button" variant="secondary" size="sm" onClick={clearModels} disabled={loading}>
   232	                    Clear
   233	                  </Button>
   234	                </div>
   235	              </div>
   236	              <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
   237	                {modelOptions.map((model) => {
   238	                  const isSelected = selectedModelIds.includes(model.value);
   239	                  return (
   240	                    <Button
   241	                      key={model.value}
   242	                      type="button"
   243	                      variant="ghost"
   244	                      size="sm"
   245	                      onClick={() => toggleModelId(model.value)}
   246	                      className={`rounded-full border px-3 py-1 text-xs font-medium transition-colors min-h-0 ${
   247	                        isSelected
   248	                          ? 'border-teal-600 bg-teal-600 text-white hover:bg-teal-700'
   249	                          : 'border-gray-300 bg-white text-gray-700 hover:border-teal-400 hover:text-teal-700 hover:bg-white'
   250	                      }`}
   251	                      title={model.label}
   252	                    >
   253	                      {model.label}
   254	                    </Button>
   255	                  );
   256	                })}
   257	                {modelOptions.length === 0 && !loading && (
   258	                  <span className="text-sm text-gray-500">No active models are available.</span>
   259	                )}
   260	              </div>
   261	              <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">
   262	                {loading
   263	                  ? 'Loading models and domains...'
   264	                  : modelOptions.length === 0
   265	                    ? 'No active models are available.'
   266	                    : selectedModelCount === modelOptions.length
   267	                      ? 'All visible models selected.'
   268	                      : `${selectedModelCount} of ${modelOptions.length} visible models selected.`}
   269	                {singleDomainActive && selectedDomain != null && (
   270	                  <span className="ml-2">Viewing {selectedDomain.name} only.</span>
   271	                )}
   272	              </div>
   273	            </div>
   274	          </details>
   275	        </div>
   276	      </section>
   277	
   278	      {loading && <Loading size="lg" text="Loading models analysis..." />}
   279	
   280	      {!loading && (
   281	        <ModelsMatrix
   282	          models={models}
   283	          selectedModelIds={selectedModelIds}
   284	          singleDomainActive={singleDomainActive}
   285	          selectedCellKey={selectedCell == null ? null : `${selectedCell.modelId}:${selectedCell.valueKey}`}
   286	          onCellClick={handleCellClick}
   287	        />
   288	      )}
   289	
   290	      <ModelValueDetailDrawer
   291	        open={selectedCell != null}
   292	        model={selectedModel}
   293	        value={selectedValue}
   294	        onClose={() => setSelectedCell(null)}
   295	      />
   296	    </div>
   297	  );
   298	}

codex
## Findings
- High [CODE-CONFIRMED] FR-022 is not feasible against the current persisted aggregate output. The aggregate pipeline writes only summary fields to the analysis result (`perModel`, `preferenceSummary`, `reliabilitySummary`, `aggregateMetadata`, `modelAgreement`, `visualizationData`, `mostContestedScenarios`, `varianceAnalysis`, `decisionStats`, `valueAggregateStats`, plus metadata), and it does not persist the transcript-level `AggregateWorkerTranscript[]` or raw definition content needed to rebuild the `(own × opponent)` grid or run FR-018 validation later. See [aggregate-run-workflow.ts](/Users/chrislaw/valuerank/.claude/worktrees/great-noyce-ed9f59/cloud/apps/api/src/services/analysis/aggregate/aggregate-run-workflow.ts#L154) and [contracts.ts](/Users/chrislaw/valuerank/.claude/worktrees/great-noyce-ed9f59/cloud/apps/api/src/services/analysis/aggregate/contracts.ts#L197). As written, the report cannot be sourced from AGGREGATE records alone.

- Medium [CODE-CONFIRMED] FR-002b asks for a single canonical normalization pipeline built by importing existing helpers, but the code does not expose the needed primitive. `toComparableNumber` is private inside [scenario-metadata.ts](/Users/chrislaw/valuerank/.claude/worktrees/great-noyce-ed9f59/cloud/apps/api/src/services/analysis/scenario-metadata.ts#L35), and `getLevelNormalizationMap` only does exact-match string mapping with no trimming or numeric-equivalence handling in [scenarios-utils.ts](/Users/chrislaw/valuerank/.claude/worktrees/great-noyce-ed9f59/cloud/apps/api/src/graphql/queries/scenarios-utils.ts#L58). Implementing the spec without either exporting new helpers or duplicating the logic is not possible.

- Medium [UNVERIFIED] FR-024 introduces a new exclusion reason `(g) missing value-pair tokens`, but FR-018’s validation taxonomy only defines reasons `(a)` through `(f)`. That leaves two conflicting exclusion enums in the same spec, so downstream counting/reporting logic will not have one authoritative reason set unless FR-018 is expanded.

## Residual Risks
- The spec still does not pin down whether the new page inherits the existing Models page filters (`domain`, batch/signature, model selection) or runs across the full roster. That choice changes the denominator for every headline number.

- The default metric for the 2D heat map is still open, and the page-level summary visualization is also unspecified. If the team picks the wrong default, the report can read inconsistently even if the math is correct.

- Mirrored value pairs are canonicalized in FR-024, but the spec does not say what happens when one side of a mirror is valid and the other is excluded. That can skew coverage counts and make the “measured vs excluded” totals hard to trust.
